panel.title: Connected Devices

card.network-description: Reported by the phone connectivity plugin.
card.signal-description: No live cellular reading is available.

device-status.select-ready: Switch panel controls to this phone
device-status.open-phone: Open KDE Connect on the phone or keep both devices on the same network
device-status.pair-first: Pair this device before mirror and file actions are available

panel.send-file-picker: Pick file to send to device

quick-actions.title: Quick actions
quick-actions.description: Common phone tools without leaving the panel.
quick-actions.wireless-adb-description: Pair or reconnect adb for taps and key input.
quick-actions.browse-description: Open the phone storage in your file manager.
quick-actions.send-description: Push files directly to the active device.
quick-actions.find-description: Make the phone ring so you can find it.

remote.title: Live control
remote.description: Tap, drag, and use Android navigation directly from the panel.
remote.connected-description: KDE Connect is active. Quick actions and remote control stay in one place.

wireless-adb.missing-command-description: The built-in Wireless ADB helper could not start.
wireless-adb.missing-pair-parameters-description: Enter the phone IP, pairing port, and pairing code
wireless-adb.missing-connect-parameters-description: Enter the phone IP and connect port
wireless-adb.missing-connect-host-description: Select a reachable KDE Connect phone first so the plugin knows which host to scan.
wireless-adb.dialog-description: Pair from Android's Wireless debugging screen, then connect with the current adb port shown on the phone.
wireless-adb.random-port-note: Wireless debugging ports can change each time. Enter the current port shown on the phone.
wireless-adb.qr-section-description: On the phone, open Wireless debugging and choose Pair device with QR code, then scan this image.
wireless-adb.qr-placeholder: Tap Start QR to generate a pairing code.
wireless-adb.qr-helper-description: The plugin will wait for the scan, pair automatically, then connect ADB for the selected phone.
wireless-adb.qr-footer-description: Leave this popup open until the phone finishes the scan.
wireless-adb.qr-waiting-description: Waiting for the phone to scan the QR code and publish its pairing service.
wireless-adb.qr-success-description: Wireless ADB paired and connected from the QR code.
wireless-adb.qr-generate-error-description: Failed to generate the Wireless ADB QR code.
wireless-adb.missing-qr-parameters-description: Generate a fresh Wireless ADB QR code and try again.
wireless-adb.pair-section-description: On the phone, open Wireless debugging and choose Pair device with pairing code.
wireless-adb.connect-section-description: Use the current adb port shown on the phone. Android may change this port after reconnecting Wireless debugging.
wireless-adb.auto-connect-running-description: Scanning for the selected phone's current Wireless ADB port...
wireless-adb.auto-connect-success-description: Wireless ADB connected using the detected current port.
wireless-adb.legacy-section-description: This runs the configured helper command, such as adb tcpip 5555, for older or already-authorized ADB flows.
wireless-adb.running-description: Keep this panel open until adb finishes.

embedded-mirror.starting-description: Launching scrcpy and preparing the live feed.
embedded-mirror.ready-description: Tap, drag, and use the Android navigation bar below.

scrcpy.running-description: Click the phone tile again to stop the session
scrcpy.adb-missing-description: Install Android platform-tools so the plugin can use adb for mirroring and input.
scrcpy.adb-authorize-description: Enable Developer options and USB debugging on the phone, connect it over USB, unlock it, and accept the USB debugging prompt for this computer.
scrcpy.adb-offline-description: adb can see the phone, but it is not ready yet. Reconnect the cable, unlock the phone, and accept the USB debugging prompt again.
scrcpy.adb-setup-description: Connect one authorized ADB phone over USB or Wireless ADB to use mirroring and input.
scrcpy.adb-setup-wireless-description: Connect ADB for the selected phone. The panel will not reuse another phone's ADB session.
scrcpy.not-configured-description: Set a scrcpy command in the plugin settings
scrcpy.missing-command-description: Set a scrcpy command in the plugin settings

panel.pair-needed-subtitle: KDE Connect reported this device as temporarily unpaired.
panel.pair-requested-subtitle: Approve the request on the phone to restore controls.
panel.pair-requested: Confirm the pairing request on the phone. The mirror and device actions will come back automatically after approval.
panel.pair-description: This device is temporarily reported as unpaired. Retry pairing here if KDE Connect did not recover on its own after reconnecting.
panel.pair-waiting: Waiting for the phone to accept the pairing request.

diagnostics.dialog-description: Raw connection state for KDE Connect, ADB, scrcpy, and the embedded video feed.
diagnostics.kde-reachable-body: is paired but unreachable. Select another reachable device or keep the phone awake on the same network.
diagnostics.adb-only-body: KDE Connect is not paired, but an authorized ADB device is available for mirroring and input.
diagnostics.adb-waiting-body: Waiting for KDE Connect reachability before checking ADB controls.
diagnostics.adb-ready-body: An authorized ADB transport is available for the selected phone.
diagnostics.v4l2-ready-body: is present and writable for the embedded video feed.
diagnostics.v4l2-missing-body: is missing or not writable. Copy the loopback setup command below if you want the embedded feed.
diagnostics.scrcpy-ready-body: The embedded mirror command is configured and will launch after the required transports are ready.

kdeconnect-error.no-devices: No device running KDE Connect found
kdeconnect-error.unavailable-title: kdeconnectd does not seem to be running!
kdeconnect-error.unavailable-desc: Make sure you've installed the KDE Connect Application on your system and that it has started the kdeconnectd daemon

busctl-error.unavailable-desc: Make sure busctl (part of systemd) is installed on your system

iconColor.label: Widget Icon Color
iconColor.desc: Color of the AndroidConnect bar widget icon
